channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Many Ways Software Engineers Can Go Independent
item.description: When I talk about software engineers becoming solopreneurs, it’s typically interpreted in one of these ways: You bootstrap a small SaaS or development tool product. You start freelancing. You...

item.title: Big Tech firms are accelerating AI investments and integration, while regulators and companies focus on safety and responsible adoption.
item.description: The AI landscape is experiencing unprecedented growth and transformation. This post delves into the key developments shaping the future of artificial intelligence, from massive industry...

item.title: Hands-On with nylas ui: Start web configuration UI
item.description: The nylas ui command launches a local web-based configuration interface in your browser. The nylas ui command launches a local web-based configuration interface in your browser. Manage connected...

item.title: AI Agents Need a Constitution: The Missing Control Layer Google Cloud NEXT ‘26 Didn’t Solve
item.description: This is a submission for the Google Cloud NEXT Writing Challenge AI Agents Need a Constitution: The Missing Control Layer Google Cloud NEXT ‘26 Didn’t Solve At Google Cloud NEXT ‘26, one thing...

item.title: nylas calendar events list: Developer Reference with Examples
item.description: Calendar management belongs in the terminal too. nylas calendar events list works with Google Calendar, Outlook, and Exchange. The nylas calendar events list command displays upcoming events from...

item.title: Working with nylas email search: Search emails by query and filters
item.description: Search emails by query and filters. Works across Gmail, Outlook, Exchange, Yahoo, iCloud, and IMAP. The nylas email search command finds emails matching a query across your connected mailbox....

item.title: nylas email read for Developers â Quick Reference
item.description: Working with email from the terminal means you don't need to open Gmail or Outlook. nylas email read does the job. The nylas email read command displays the full content of a specific email by ID,...

item.title: List recent emails with optional filters from the Command Line
item.description: Scripts, cron jobs, and AI agents all need email access. nylas email list provides it without SMTP configuration. The nylas email list command fetches recent emails from your connected mailbox and...

item.title: Remote Kiro: A Serverless Control Plane for Kiro
item.description: I liked the autonomous agent experience in Kiro, but I kept wishing for one extra thing. I wanted the agent to run on my own machine, use my own tools, and work inside my own environment. At the...

item.title: The Question We Are All Asking About AI Has Changed
item.description: A couple of years ago, the dominant question in every engineering meeting, every Slack thread, every developer blog was: which model is the best? People ran benchmarks. They argued about MMLU...

item.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
item.description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

item.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
item.description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

item.title: Trump fires the entire National Science Board
item.description: Multiple sources are reporting that the Trump administration has dismissed the entire National Science Board (NSB). The NSB advises the president and Congress on the National Science Foundation...

item.title: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices
item.description: Part of what has held back electric cars has been the cost. But an influx of used vehicles over the next three years could bring prices down dramatically. In 2025, just 123,000 leases on EVs...

item.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
item.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign...

item.title: Researchers say we’re talking less than ever
item.description: Researchers at the University of Missouri-Kansas City and the University of Arizona say that between 2005 and 2019, the number of words we speak out loud to another human being fell by nearly 28...

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding...

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: Sharge’s fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
item.description: I’ve been testing compact, magnetic Qi2 power banks that can snap onto your phone for an upcoming buying guide. They make recharging much easier than bringing along a huge battery that weighs down...

item.title: The Govee smart lamp brightened up my room, and then my life
item.description: I knew things were not quite right when I had to throw a towel over a broken Ikea lamp to block out its light. How did I get here? I cover fancy and capable tech for a living, and yet, it took me...

item.title: Xbox’s weirdest studio is on a roll
item.description: For a while there, it seemed like Double Fine might be struggling under the Microsoft corporate umbrella. The game studio led by Tim Schafer is beloved for offbeat titles like Brütal Legend and...

item.title: Sho Miyake answers life’s greatest questions
item.description: Acclaimed Japanese director Sho Miyake has arrived in the States. He's brought with him two feature films: Small, Slow But Steady and Two Seasons, Two Strangers, a pair of naturalistic portraits...

item.title: The most exciting laptop I’ve seen in forever
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 125, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, send me cereal recommendations, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: The US gets the worst phones
item.description: Apple and Samsung dominate the US phone market, and they've done so for years. Together with Google, they've shaped our sense of what a smartphone is and what it can do, pushing the boundaries of...

item.title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

item.title: Ace the Ping-Pong Robot Can Whup Your Ass
item.description: Ace can read the trajectory of a ball, adjust the racket angle, and respond with strokes that keep the exchange alive with real players.

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment...

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its...

item.title: Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters
item.description: On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model. Notably, the model can process much longer prompts than its last generation, thanks to a new...
